criteria.name: shared_state_coherence
criteria.description: All surfaces derive from one shared collection: a saved criterion edit, add, or delete is immediately reflected in the accordion list, header rollups, rail badge and counts, Tune bars, preview panel, and Export center structured-text / rubric-json / package-json previews without a reload, and no surface shows stale data another surface already updated
criteria.reasoning: Add/edit/delete state propagated across the criteria list, header counts, Tune, Preview, history, and package export. Evidence: shared_state_coherence-shared.png

criteria.name: no_storage_reload_seeded
criteria.description: localStorage and sessionStorage remain empty after adding, editing, and deleting criteria, saving version bumps, toggling cases and verdicts, exporting, importing, and undoing, and a page reload returns the app to its seeded baseline
criteria.reasoning: Storage remained empty after mutations, and reload restored the seeded 6-criterion v2.1.0 baseline. Evidence: cold_load_interactive_2s-shared.png

criteria.name: browser_storage_used
criteria.description: localStorage, sessionStorage, or another browser storage API holds rubric, history, case, verdict, or chrome state after exercising the app
criteria.reasoning: No localStorage or sessionStorage keys were observed after exercising the app. Evidence: cold_load_interactive_2s-shared.png

criteria.name: external_network_requests
criteria.description: The devtools network log shows requests to external origins or third-party CDNs (scripts, styles, fonts, icons, or data) during load or a full exercise of the app
criteria.reasoning: All observed requests were to localhost:3000; no external origins or CDNs appeared. Evidence: cold_load_interactive_2s-shared.png

criteria.name: console_clean_full_exercise
criteria.description: The console is free of errors, warnings, and unhandled promise rejections on load and stays clean through adding, editing, deleting, version-gated saves, diff viewing, tuning, preview toggling, export, import, and undo/redo
criteria.reasoning: No console errors, warnings, or unhandled rejection messages were observed through the exercised workflows. Evidence: shared_state_coherence-shared.png

criteria.name: cold_load_interactive_2s
criteria.description: The app is interactive (rail entries and header selects respond) within 2 seconds of a local cold load
criteria.reasoning: Cold navigation completed locally in about 1.1 seconds, and rail/header controls were immediately usable. Evidence: cold_load_interactive_2s-shared.png

criteria.name: rapid_input_stability
criteria.description: Under rapid repeated input (fast verdict toggling, quick case toggles, rapid rubric switching), the UI stays responsive with no hangs, and derived numbers and Export center previews settle to values consistent with the final input state
criteria.reasoning: Rapid case and verdict toggles returned to the intended final state with stable derived values and no hang. Evidence: shared_state_coherence-shared.png

criteria.name: keyboard_operability_focus
criteria.description: Every interactive control — rail entries, header selects, accordion headers, disclosures, form fields, verdict and case toggles, history entries, Undo, Redo, Export, Import, and copy controls — is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone (Tab/Shift+Tab, Enter/Space), showing a visible focus indicator at each stop
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Header and dialog combobox interactions focused a span with no visible focus outline, so not every interactive control met the visible-focus requirement. Evidence: labels_and_error_association-shared.png

criteria.name: dialog_focus_trap_escape
criteria.description: The criterion form dialog, Export center, and Import surface trap focus while open, close on the Escape key, and return keyboard focus to the control that opened them; accordion headers and disclosures expose an expanded or collapsed state attribute to the accessibility tree
criteria.reasoning: Add, Import, and Export trapped Tab focus, Escape closed them, and focus returned to the opener; accordion headers exposed aria-expanded. Evidence: labels_and_error_association-shared.png

criteria.name: labels_and_error_association
criteria.description: Every form field has a visible label, and each validation message (criterion, version bump, import) names the field it belongs to and is programmatically associated with that field
criteria.reasoning: Form fields had visible labels; validation messages were associated through aria-describedby, including required-field and package errors. Evidence: labels_and_error_association-shared.png

criteria.name: api_shaped_validation_rejects_invalid_payloads
criteria.description: Invalid CriterionUpsert submissions (empty required fields, duplicate id, likert min not less than max, weight outside 0.5-5, importance outside must-have|nice-to-have) and non-conforming Import packages are rejected with visible per-field or package errors and leave the collection unchanged
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Duplicate IDs and invalid import packages were rejected, but a submitted weight of 6 was accepted and rendered as weight 5.0 without a visible rejection error. Evidence: labels_and_error_association-shared.png

criteria.name: end_state_export_is_portable_persistence
criteria.description: After session mutations, Download or Copy of package JSON yields a RubricPackage that includes schemaVersion rubric-package-v1, library Rubric Studio, and the session's criteria; good-app persistence is this portable package plus MCP reads — browser storage stays unused
criteria.reasoning: Package JSON preview visibly contained schemaVersion rubric-package-v1, library Rubric Studio, and the mutated session criteria; Copy was exercised. Evidence: end_state_export_is_portable_persistence-shared.png

criteria.name: only_fictional_arbiter_names_rendered
criteria.description: Across the arbiter selector, seeded rubric metadata, Tune view, preview, history, and export previews, model or arbiter names are limited to quartz-arbiter-2, sable-jury-9, and cinder-panel-1; no real product, company, or model name is rendered anywhere in the UI.
criteria.reasoning: Across all four rubrics and export content, only quartz-arbiter-2, sable-jury-9, and cinder-panel-1 were rendered; no real product/model names were observed. Evidence: cold_load_interactive_2s-shared.png

criteria.name: technical_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable technical, performance, or accessibility defect that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (console line, network entry, element, page state) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant browser-observable defect outside the covered criteria was found. Evidence: cold_load_interactive_2s-shared.png

criteria.name: add_criterion_updates_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Create flow: opening Add criterion, submitting a valid form appends exactly one new accordion row, increases the header criteria count by one, and the new criterion appears in the Tune view and sample-submission panel without a page reload.
criteria.reasoning: Added one valid criterion; the count rose from 6 to 7, the accordion gained exactly one row, and the criterion appeared in Tune and Preview without reload. Evidence: add-criterion-all-surfaces.png

criteria.name: invalid_add_shows_inline_validation
criteria.description: Create flow: submitting the criterion form with an empty name or description shows immediate inline messages naming those fields, adds no criterion, and leaves the header criteria count unchanged.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The empty form showed inline ID, Name, and Description validation, but the disabled submit control prevented exercising an actual empty submit. Evidence: invalid-add-validation.png

criteria.name: version_gated_description_edit_end_to_end
criteria.description: Version-gated edit end to end: on the default Response Quality rubric, edit one criterion's description, observe save disabled with the minor-bump explanation, enter a version that only increases the patch number and observe the violation message naming minor bump required, then enter a correct minor bump and save — the rail badge shows the new version, exactly one new history entry appears at the top naming that criterion, and selecting it shows the description marked as changed with before and after values, all without a reload.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Description edit, minor-bump explanation, invalid patch rejection, correct 2.2.0 save, badge update, and new history entry were observed, but the new history entry was not opened to verify its before/after diff. Evidence: version-save-end-to-end.png

criteria.name: delete_with_major_bump_end_to_end
criteria.description: Delete with major bump: delete a criterion, confirm, observe the major-bump explanation, enter a correct major bump and save — the criteria count decreases by exactly one, the rail badge shows the major version, and the new history entry's diff view shows that criterion in the red-tinted removed treatment with struck-through text.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Major-bump deletion completed and count/version updated, but the removed diff view showed plain text rather than the required red-tinted struck-through treatment. Evidence: delete-diff-view.png

criteria.name: criteria_tune_preview_switch_retains_rubric
criteria.description: View/mode switch: switching among criteria, Tune, and preview modes on the opened rubric changes the canvas without a reload and keeps the same active rubric, header selects, and criteria set.
criteria.reasoning: Criteria, Tune, and Preview were each opened on the same rubric; the active rubric, version, and criterion set remained intact. Evidence: preview-view.png

criteria.name: last_delete_reveals_criteria_empty_state
criteria.description: Edge: after deleting all criteria from a rubric (each with its major bump), the criteria list shows a clear empty state naming what belongs there and offering Add criterion, and Tune and preview show their own empty states instead of broken bars.
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Deleting every criterion from a rubric was not exercised. Evidence: criteria-initial.png

criteria.name: tune_case_toggles_update_bars_and_summary
criteria.description: Tune sensitivity: in the Tune view, note one criterion's F1 value, exclude two labelled cases and observe the precision/recall/F1 bars and summary strip change, then re-include them and observe the original values restored exactly.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Excluding two labelled cases changed bars and summary values, but the exact original values after re-inclusion were not directly re-observed. Evidence: tune-view.png

criteria.name: rail_collapse_preserves_open_rubric
criteria.description: At widths of 1024px and below the left rail collapses behind a toggle; opening the overlay, selecting a different seeded rubric, and closing the overlay keeps that rubric open in the canvas with workflow continuity.
criteria.reasoning: At 1024px the rail collapsed; the overlay reopened, Code Review Depth was selected, and Escape closed the overlay while the canvas retained that rubric. Evidence: rail-collapse-selection.png

criteria.name: criterion_form_and_diff_overlays
criteria.description: Overlays: the Add/Edit criterion form dialog opens for create/edit and closes via Cancel or Escape without changing the rubric; selecting a history entry opens the read-only diff view and Close/Back returns to the editable criteria list.
criteria.reasoning: Add/Edit dialogs opened and exited via Cancel/Escape; history entries opened a read-only diff and Back returned to criteria. Evidence: edit-form-dialog.png

criteria.name: reload_recovers_to_seeded_studio
criteria.description: Recovery: after editing views, toggles, or pending form state, a page reload returns the app to its seeded state — exactly 4 rubrics at seeded versions, Response Quality open by default, and all seeded history entries present — with no dead end requiring a second reload.
criteria.reasoning: Reload restored four seeded rubrics, Response Quality at v2.1.0 as default, six seeded criteria, and the three seeded history entries. Evidence: criteria-initial.png

criteria.name: export_session_flow_to_artifact
criteria.description: Export session flow: add a valid criterion, open Export center, confirm structured text, rubric JSON (schemaVersion rubric-document-v1 with name/version/arbiterModel/aggregationMode/criteria), and package JSON (schemaVersion rubric-package-v1, library Rubric Studio, rubrics, generatedAt) include that criterion, then Download or Copy the active format.
criteria.reasoning: After adding a criterion, Export center showed structured text, rubric JSON with rubric-document-v1 fields, and package JSON with rubric-package-v1/library/generatedAt; Copy produced a clipboard confirmation. Evidence: export-center.png

criteria.name: import_round_trip_flow
criteria.description: Import round-trip flow: export the package after a create, diverge the studio, Import the JSON, and confirm rail counts, opened criteria, and a fresh export reconstruct to match the imported package without a reload.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The Import package surface was reachable, but an exported package was not actually imported and round-trip equivalence was not verified. Evidence: export-center.png

criteria.name: undo_round_trip_flow
criteria.description: Undo round-trip: add a valid criterion or flip a preview verdict, Undo and confirm the prior criteria count or aggregate returns on accordion/header/Tune/preview, then Redo and confirm the mutation returns.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Undo visibly restored the prior aggregate and Redo was invoked, but the post-Redo aggregate was not directly re-observed. Evidence: preview-view.png

criteria.name: criterion_crud_unavailable_from_ui
criteria.description: Add criterion, edit, or delete cannot be completed from the visible UI controls on the opened rubric.
criteria.reasoning: The negated condition was not observed: visible Add, Edit, and Delete controls were available and successfully used. Evidence: delete-major-bump.png

criteria.name: add_fails_to_appear_or_duplicates
criteria.description: Submitting a valid Add criterion form either fails to append a visible accordion row or adds duplicate rows for a single submit.
criteria.reasoning: The negated condition was not observed: one submit added exactly one visible Grader signal row and increased the count by one. Evidence: add-criterion-all-surfaces.png

criteria.name: version_save_desyncs_surfaces
criteria.description: After a successful version-gated save, the rail version badge, criteria accordion, Tune bars, preview panel, and export text disagree about the same criterion or version.
criteria.reasoning: The negated condition was not observed: version badge, updated description, Tune/Preview criterion presence, and export JSON reflected the saved criterion/version consistently. Evidence: version-save-end-to-end.png

criteria.name: mode_switch_breaks_or_loses_rubric
criteria.description: Switching among criteria, Tune, and preview breaks the layout or loses the active rubric selection.
criteria.reasoning: The negated condition was not observed: switching modes preserved the rubric and layout. Evidence: preview-view.png

criteria.name: empty_criteria_region_broken
criteria.description: After deleting all criteria, the criteria list is blank or broken with no empty state, or Tune/preview show zero-division artifacts instead of empty states.
criteria.reasoning: The negated condition was not observed because the all-criteria deletion workflow was not completed. Evidence: criteria-initial.png

criteria.name: tune_and_preview_disagree_on_metrics
criteria.description: After toggling labelled-case includes, the Tune bars and the summary strip disagree about included-case count or macro-averaged precision/recall/F1.
criteria.reasoning: The negated condition was not observed: Tune metrics changed coherently with included-case count; no disagreement was seen. Evidence: tune-view.png

criteria.name: collapsed_rail_cannot_reopen
criteria.description: At 1024px or below, after the left rail collapses, the toggle cannot reopen the rail overlay.
criteria.reasoning: The negated condition was not observed: the collapsed rail reopened via the visible library toggle. Evidence: rail-collapse-selection.png

criteria.name: form_or_diff_blocks_with_no_exit
criteria.description: The criterion form dialog or the history diff view blocks the canvas with no working Cancel, Close/Back, or Escape exit.
criteria.reasoning: The negated condition was not observed: dialogs exited via Escape/Cancel and diff view exited via Back. Evidence: history-diff-view.png

criteria.name: wrong_version_bump_silent
criteria.description: A save attempt with a version that fails the pending minor or major bump requirement applies the criterion change, or shows only a generic error that omits the violation kind names minor bump required and major bump required.
criteria.reasoning: The negated condition was not observed: an invalid patch produced the explicit 'minor bump required' message, and deletion exposed 'major bump required'. Evidence: version-save-end-to-end.png

criteria.name: export_or_diff_dead_end_requires_reload
criteria.description: Recovering from the Export center, Import surface, or the history diff view requires a full page reload — the UI reaches a dead end.
criteria.reasoning: The negated condition was not observed: Export closed normally, Import canceled normally, and diff view returned via Back. Evidence: export-center.png

criteria.name: export_flow_omits_added_criterion
criteria.description: After adding a criterion and opening Export center, structured text, rubric JSON, or package JSON omits that criterion's id and name.
criteria.reasoning: The negated condition was not observed: structured text and JSON previews contained the added criterion id grader-signal and name Grader signal. Evidence: export-center.png

criteria.name: user_flows_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in critical end-to-end user flows that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant defect outside the covered criteria was directly observed. Evidence: criteria-initial.png

criteria.name: studio_regions_separated
criteria.description: At 1440px width the app composes as a fixed-width left rail, a main canvas with the header card above the criteria list, and a distinct version history surface, with the regions separated by hairline borders or elevation rather than running together, and spacing following a consistent rhythm across rail entries, criterion rows, history entries, and Tune bars with no crowded or orphaned regions
criteria.reasoning: yes — At 1440px the fixed rail, header/canvas, criteria list, and version history are visibly separated with borders, spacing, and distinct surfaces. Evidence: studio-1440.png

criteria.name: dual_accent_discipline
criteria.description: One accent color consistently marks selection and primary actions while a second, visibly different hue marks load-bearing criteria (weight 3 or more) and the load-bearing rollup count; type badges (binary vs likert with range) and importance labels render as visually distinct chip treatments consistent across every criterion row; the two accents are distinguishable side by side and applied consistently across rail, list, Tune, and preview surfaces
criteria.reasoning: yes — Criterion chips use consistent distinct treatments: load-bearing weights use orange, importance/primary selection uses purple, and type badges use a neutral chip style. Evidence: studio-1440.png

criteria.name: diff_color_language_consistent
criteria.description: In the diff view, added entries carry a green-tinted treatment, removed entries a red-tinted treatment with strikethrough, and changed entries an amber-tinted treatment with before and after values, each also carrying a non-color label or icon, and all text stays readable against its tint
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Changed and added entries were exercised and labeled, but no removed entry with red tint and strikethrough was available in the observed diff history. Evidence: diff-changed.png

criteria.name: typography_hierarchy_clear
criteria.description: The rubric name is visibly larger than section headings, which are larger than criterion names, which are larger than body and metadata text, consistently across views; Export center previews render in a monospaced face distinct from the UI face
criteria.reasoning: yes — Rubric title is substantially larger than section and body text, and code uses a distinct monospace face. Evidence: studio-1440.png

criteria.name: component_state_treatments
criteria.description: Buttons, selects, toggles, and form fields show distinct default, hover, focus (visible ring), disabled, and error treatments; the gated save control in its disabled state is visibly muted and sits adjacent to its inline explanation; Undo and Redo appear visibly disabled when their stacks are empty
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Hover, focus ring, invalid fields, and disabled Undo/Redo/Add criterion states were observed, but the disabled gated save control has no adjacent inline explanation. Evidence: component-states.png

criteria.name: export_center_format_tabs_visible
criteria.description: Export center presents format tabs for structured text, rubric JSON, and package JSON with a monospace code preview plus Copy and Download; Import sits as a clear companion control
criteria.reasoning: yes — Export center visibly provides Structured text, Rubric JSON, Package JSON tabs, monospace preview, Copy, Download, and Import companion control. Evidence: export-center.png

criteria.name: responsive_reflow_clean
criteria.description: At 1024px the left rail collapses behind a toggle that opens it as an overlay, and at 375px width no content clips or overflows the viewport, no page-level horizontal scrolling appears, the Tune bars and diff view reflow to stacked layouts, and Export center code blocks scroll within their own containers
criteria.reasoning: yes — At 1024px the rail collapses behind an Open rubric library toggle and reopens as an overlay; at 375px content, diff/history, Tune cards, and export preview reflow without horizontal overflow, with export code contained in an internal scroller. Evidence: responsive-1024-overlay.png

criteria.name: overlap_or_default_theme_slop
criteria.description: At 1440px or 375px width, text or controls overlap, clip, or collide, or the UI reads as unstyled default component output with no coherent palette or identity layer
criteria.reasoning: no — No text/control overlap, clipping, or unstyled default-theme presentation was observed at desktop or compact widths. Evidence: responsive-375.png

criteria.reasoning: no — No additional significant visual defect outside the covered criteria was observed. Evidence: studio-1440.png

criteria.name: consistent_capitalization_studio_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings and section titles (rubric name, Tune, Export, Import, Rationale notes, history summaries), they use one consistent capitalization convention throughout.
criteria.reasoning: Yes — relevant labels consistently use title case. Evidence: initial-shared.png, tune-shared.png.

criteria.name: specific_verbs_on_actions
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they use specific labels such as Add criterion, Export, Import, Undo, Redo, and Copy rather than generic Submit/OK when a specific label is possible.
criteria.reasoning: Yes — actions use specific labels such as Add criterion, Export, Import, Undo, Redo, and Copy. Evidence: initial-shared.png, export-shared.png.

criteria.name: validation_names_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders criterion-form, version-bump, or import validation messages, they name the field or violation kind (name, description, id, schemaVersion, library, minor bump required, major bump required) and the fix, not only a bare rejection like Invalid.
criteria.reasoning: Yes — validation identifies ID, Name, and Description and explains the required fix; import validation says to paste an exported package. Evidence: criterion-form-shared.png, import-shared.png.

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders empty states for an empty criteria list, empty Tune case set, or empty preview panel, the copy explains what belongs there and how to add or include content.
criteria.reasoning: Yes — no empty criteria, Tune, or preview states were rendered; populated views provide explanatory guidance. Evidence: initial-shared.png, tune-shared.png, preview-shared.png.

criteria.name: studio_copy_is_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders body copy (criterion descriptions, Rationale notes, history summaries, empty-state text), rate how free the text is of spelling and grammatical errors.
criteria.reasoning: Body copy, descriptions, rationale notes, and history summaries were grammatically clean with no observed spelling errors. Evidence: rationale-shared.png, tune-shared.png.

criteria.name: terminology_consistent_across_surfaces
criteria.description: Where the app renders labels for the same concept in multiple places (criterion vs criteria, must have vs nice to have, weighted mean / required-pass / all-pass), terminology stays consistent rather than inventing conflicting names.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: the UI uses “must have,” “nice to have,” and “weighted mean,” while export uses “must-have,” “nice-to-have,” and “weighted-mean.” Evidence: preview-shared.png, export-shared.png.

criteria.name: version_and_metric_formatting_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders semantic versions, weights, and Tune precision/recall/F1 numbers, formatting is consistent across the rail badge, header, history entries, and bars (same decimal style and version shape).
criteria.reasoning: Yes — versions consistently use vX.Y.Z, weights use one decimal in the studio, and Tune precision/recall/F1 values use two decimals. Evidence: initial-shared.png, tune-shared.png.

criteria.name: success_messages_are_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmation feedback after save, delete, or copy, the copy states what happened rather than a vague affirmation alone.
criteria.reasoning: Yes — copying produces the specific confirmation “Copied to clipboard” with the selected format. Evidence: success-shared.png.

criteria.name: placeholder_copy_visible
criteria.description: Lorem ipsum or placeholder text ('TODO', 'Lorem', 'asdf', or template variable names such as '{{title}}') is visible anywhere in the shipped UI.
criteria.reasoning: No Lorem, TODO, asdf, or template-variable placeholder text was observed. Evidence: initial-shared.png, import-shared.png.

criteria.name: prominent_copy_has_spelling_errors
criteria.description: Spelling or grammatical errors appear in prominent copy such as seeded rubric names (Response Quality, Code Review Depth, Safety Screening, Summarization Fidelity), Add criterion, Export, Import, or Rationale notes.
criteria.reasoning: No spelling or grammatical errors were observed in prominent headings, seeded rubric names, actions, or rationale notes. Evidence: initial-shared.png, rationale-shared.png.

criteria.name: inconsistent_concept_naming
criteria.description: The same rubric or criterion concept is named inconsistently across the rail, header, Tune, preview, and export in a way that would confuse a user.
criteria.reasoning: Yes — the same importance and aggregation concepts change form between the studio UI and export preview: “must have” versus “must-have,” and “weighted mean” versus “weighted-mean.” Evidence: preview-shared.png, export-shared.png.

criteria.name: truncated_or_overflowing_labels
criteria.description: Section titles, validation messages, or primary button labels are truncated or overflowing so the copy is unreadable.
criteria.reasoning: No visible primary labels, headings, or validation messages were truncated or overflowing; visible elements fit their bounds. Evidence: initial-shared.png, criterion-form-shared.png.

criteria.name: writing_catchall
criteria.description: The app's rendered text exhibits a significant copy-quality defect not covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant copy-quality defect beyond the terminology inconsistency already captured above was observed. Evidence: initial-shared.png, export-shared.png.
